challenges.title: Block, Inline, and Inline-Block
challenges.description: Write three CSS rules: (1) target `.full-width` and set `display` to `block`, (2) target `.inline-text` and set `display` to `inline`, (3) target `.badge` and set `display` to `inline-block` with `padding` of `4px 8px`. Assign your CSS to the variable `css`.
challenges.test_code:
assert '.full-width' in css, 'Should target .full-width'
assert '.inline-text' in css, 'Should target .inline-text'
assert '.badge' in css, 'Should target .badge'
assert 'block' in css.lower(), 'Should use display: block'
assert 'inline-block' in css.lower(), 'Should use display: inline-block'
assert 'display' in css.lower(), 'Should set the display property'
assert css.lower().count('display') >= 3, 'Should set display in all three rules'
assert 'padding' in css.lower(), 'Should set padding on .badge'
hints: block takes full width; inline flows with text (no width/height); inline-block flows inline but accepts width/height/padding.
Use `display: inline-block;` to create pill-shaped badges that sit inline with text.

challenges.title: Display None vs Visibility Hidden
challenges.description: Write two CSS rules: (1) target `.removed` and set `display` to `none` — this removes the element from the layout entirely, (2) target `.invisible` and set `visibility` to `hidden` — this hides the element but it still occupies space. Assign your CSS to the variable `css`.
challenges.test_code:
assert '.removed' in css, 'Should target .removed'
assert '.invisible' in css, 'Should target .invisible'
assert 'display' in css.lower(), 'Should use the display property'
assert 'none' in css.lower(), 'Should use display: none'
assert 'visibility' in css.lower(), 'Should use the visibility property'
assert 'hidden' in css.lower(), 'Should use visibility: hidden'
hints: display: none removes the element from the flow — surrounding elements close the gap.
visibility: hidden makes the element invisible but it still takes up its original space in the layout.
